  - Row 12: BBBBBBBbbbbbBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBRBB (player room + red path)

- After RIGHT (no SPACE): first=blue, second=gray (back to initial?)

This is confusing. Let me re-examine the indicator squares more carefully.

- Second square: gray

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at cols 20-24, try SPACE
result = action(['SPACE'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24:
        print(f"After SPACE: player at {n['boundary']}")
        break
print(f"Gameplay changed: {last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check indicator squares
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 16 and n['color'] in ['b', 'w', 'g', 'G']:
        b = n['boundary']
        if b[0][0] < 6:
            print(f"Indicator: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={b}")

# Check maze
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nMaze (rows 7-13, cols 13-43):")
for i in range(7, 14):
    row = ascii_grid[i]
    print(f"Row {i:2d}: {row[13:43]}")

print("\nMaze (rows 13-25, cols 19-25):")
for i in range(13, 26):
    row = ascii_grid[i]
    print(f"Row {i:2d}: {row[19:25]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
